signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.28, trading above the 160.00 intervention watch level following the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This geopolitical de-escalation typically reduces safe-haven demand for JPY, but the 287bp Fed-BoJ carry premium (SOFR 3.60% vs TONA 0.73%) remains the primary driver for the pair's bid. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (week ending Jun 9) indicates crowded carry positioning. The BoJ's next meeting on 16 Jun is a key catalyst for any shift in yield gap direction. Intervention risk is elevated on sustained trading above 160.00.
evidence: US-Iran agreement: Strait of Hormuz reopening

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7078. COT LF net long +38,092 (week ending Jun 9) indicates significant speculative long positioning. However, the AUD CESI net −8 misses suggests data is consistently underperforming expectations, creating a divergence between speculative positioning and economic fundamentals. Options market shows elevated vol premium with IV 15.0% vs HV30 8.3% (VRP +6.7%), indicating strong hedging demand. Watch for RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun for a shift in bias or further data disappointment to trigger long unwinds.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry Neutrality
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3421. The pair exhibits an elevated volatility risk premium with IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0% (VRP +5.0%), indicating strong options hedging demand above realized volatility. This occurs despite a neutral carry differential of 0bp (SOFR 3.60% vs SONIA 3.73%) between the Fed and BoE. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 18 Jun is the immediate catalyst for any shift in policy or market expectations, which is likely to impact the elevated vol pricing.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Extreme + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3986. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 91% short bias (Myfxbook, 18:03 UTC), signaling potential contrarian reversal risk if price extends higher. This is compounded by a significant correlation break with WTI Oil (corr=−0.229 vs 252d norm=−0.019, z=−1.61σ), indicating the typical inverse relationship has weakened. The recent US-Iran deal has sent WTI Oil down 4.76% (1D), but USD/CAD has only moved -0.01% (1D), suggesting other factors are at play. Watch for sustained WTI recovery or a shift in retail positioning to invalidate the contrarian read.
evidence: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil corr: z=-1.61σ
